title: RWisecondorX Agent Guidelines
text: Build R toolkit copy number analysis trisomy prediction non-invasive prenatal testing (NIPT), comparable scope NIPTUtils built entirely Rduckhts/DuckDB rather Rsamtools/Python. package ports two upstream implementations: WisecondorX — copy number variation detection cfDNA (Python/pysam upstream) NIPTeR — fast trisomy prediction via chromosomal fraction Z-scores, NCV scores, regression, chi-squared correction (R/Rsamtools upstream) Design priorities: Exact conformance upstream implementations documented testable. Performance — DuckDB SQL replaces per-read Python loops Rsamtools scans; large BAMs run seconds, minutes. Interoperable file formats — bgzipped, tabix-indexed BED files intermediate layer binning analysis, consumable DuckDB, R, Python, tabix-aware tool. Python runtime dependency — Rduckhts replaces pysam HTS operations; reticulate condathis optional used conformance testing.

title: RWisecondorX Agent Guidelines
text: Shared binning engine — R/convert.R - bam_convert(): DuckDB/SQL read-counting core. Supports binsize, mapq, require_flags (samtools -f), exclude_flags (samtools -F), rmdup ("streaming" / "flag" / "none"), separate_strands (returns list forward/reverse data frames), CRAM via reference. Achieves exact bin--bin conformance WisecondorX HG00106.chrom11 (25,115 non-zero bins, 0 mismatches). - bam_convert_bed(): bgzipped + tabix-indexed 4-column BED output (chrom, start, end, count). Uses Rduckhts::rduckhts_bgzip() rduckhts_tabix_index() — external tools. WisecondorX layer - R/wisecondorx_cli.R: wisecondorx_convert(), wisecondorx_newref(), wisecondorx_predict() — thin condathis wrappers delegating official bioconda package. - R/wisecondorx_npz.R: bam_convert_npz() — WisecondorX-compatible NPZ output via reticulate, kept Python CLI conformance/interoperability rather native R pipeline. NIPTeR binning layer — R/nipter_bin.R - nipter_bin_bam(): produces NIPTeRSample objects. separate_strands = FALSE (default), class c("NIPTeRSample", "CombinedStrands"); separate_strands = TRUE, class c("NIPTeRSample", "SeparatedStrands") autosomal_chromosome_reads list two matrices (forward/reverse, rownames "1F".."22F" / "1R".."22R"). Exposes mapq, require_flags, exclude_flags, rmdup pre-filtering matching real-world NIPT pipelines (e.g. mapq=40L, exclude_flags=1024L). - nipter_bin_bam_bed(): BED output separate_strands support. FALSE (default): 5-column BED (chrom, start, end, count, corrected_count). TRUE: 9-column BED (chrom, start, end, count, count_fwd, count_rev, corrected_count, corrected_fwd, corrected_rev). Tests - inst/tinytest/test_fixtures.R (41 assertions): synthetic BAM/CRAM fixtures, three rmdup modes, CRAM reference round-trip. - inst/tinytest/test_nipter.R (47 assertions): NIPTeRSample structure, matrix dimensions rownames, MAPQ filter, exclude_flags=1024 dedup, BED.gz output shape, bin--bin conformance NIPTeR::bin_bam_sample() using bundled whole-genome fixture default (NIPTER_CONFORMANCE_BAM remains override). - inst/tinytest/test_cli_args.R: CLI argument builder unit tests. - inst/tinytest/test_npz.R: NPZ round-trip tests. - inst/tinytest/test_integration.R: WisecondorX bin--bin conformance via condathis. - inst/tinytest/test_sra_metadata.R: SRA URL helpers. Infrastructure - R/aaa.R: SRA metadata helpers. - scripts/make_fixtures.sh: reproducible synthetic BAM/CRAM fixture generation. - DESCRIPTION: NIPTeR authors (Dirk de Weerd, Lennart Johansson) listed cph. NIPTeR added Suggests. NIPTeR statistical layer file strictly separate; never mix NIPTeR WisecondorX code. statistical functions support CombinedStrands SeparatedStrands samples. R/nipter_control.R — nipter_as_control_group(): constructs NIPTeRControlGroup list NIPTeRSample objects validation dedup. nipter_diagnose_control_group(): per-chromosome Z-scores Shapiro-Wilk normality tests across control group. nipter_match_control_group(): selects best-fit control samples test sample; distance computation Rcpp+OpenMP nipter_ssd_scores_cpp() kernel (one query vs N columns pre-extracted 22×N fractions matrix). nipter_match_matrix(): computes full N×N pairwise SSD matrix via nipter_ssd_matrix_cpp() — replaces production lapply(1:N, match_control_group(..., mode="report")) hot loop single vectorized call. nipter_control_group_from_beds(): loads directory TSV.bgz files control group one DuckDB pass via rduckhts_tabix_multi(). Internal helpers .sample_chr_fractions() (44-element SeparatedStrands, 22 Combined) .sample_chr_fractions_collapsed() (always 22-element) handle strand dispatch. R/nipter_gc.R — nipter_gc_precompute(): runs rduckhts_fasta_nuc() writes 5-column TSV.bgz+tbi (chrom, start, end, pct_gc, seq_len); use pay FASTA scan cost per reference build rather per sample. nipter_gc_correct(): LOESS bin-weight GC correction accepting gc_table parameter — either path precomputed TSV.bgz, -memory list, NULL (falls back fasta path). correcting NIPTeRControlGroup, GC table resolved reused every sample. SeparatedStrands: LOESS fitted Reduce("+", auto_list) summed counts, corrections applied independently strand matrix via lapply(). Sex chromosome correction via nearest-neighbour (LOESS) -bucket weights (bin-weight). R/nipter_chi.R — nipter_chi_correct(): chi-squared overdispersion correction applied simultaneously sample control group. SeparatedStrands: chi computed summed strand counts, correction applied per-strand via lapply(). R/nipter_score.R — nipter_z_score(): chromosomal fraction Z-score Shapiro-Wilk normality test. Uses collapsed fractions SeparatedStrands. nipter_ncv_score(): normalised chromosome value brute-force denominator search using utils::combn() (replaces sets::set_combn() dependency). R/nipter_regression.R — nipter_regression(): forward stepwise regression Z-score train/test split, practical vs theoretical CV selection. Supports CombinedStrands SeparatedStrands. SeparatedStrands doubles predictor pool (44 candidates: "1F".."22F","1R".."22R") complementary exclusion (selecting "5F" excludes "5F" "5R" model). R/nipter_sex.R — nipter_sex_model(): 2-component GMM sex prediction via mclust::Mclust(), supporting "y_fraction" "xy_fraction" methods. nipter_sex_model_y_unique(): 2-component GMM Y-unique region read ratios (BAM-level, uses nipter_y_unique_ratio()). nipter_y_unique_ratio(): counts reads overlapping 7 Y-chromosome unique gene regions via DuckDB/duckhts index-based region queries returns ratio total nuclear reads. nipter_predict_sex(): classifies sample male/female using one models majority-vote consensus; y_unique_ratio parameter enables Y-unique model vote. Internal .mclust_fit() wrapper evaluates mclust namespace work around mclustBIC() namespace-qualified upstream mclust. inst/tinytest/test_nipter_stats.R — 105 assertions covering statistical functions including SeparatedStrands variants. inst/tinytest/test_nipter_sex.R — 45 assertions covering sex model building, Y-unique model, 3-model consensus, classification accuracy, prediction, edge cases. Native WisecondorX implementation (newref + predict) Pure R/Rcpp port WisecondorX newref predict pipelines, performance-critical KNN reference-finding compiled C++. Python runtime dependency. R/rwisecondorx_utils.R — Shared utilities: .train_gender_model() (2-component GMM Y-fractions zero-variance fallback), .gender_correct(), .get_mask() (5% median coverage threshold), .normalize_and_mask(), .train_pca() (5 components, ratio correction), .project_pc(), .predict_gender(). Also exports scale_sample() rescaling bin sizes. R/mclust_utils.R — Shared internal mclust namespace helper used NIPTeR native WisecondorX sex-model code paths. R/rwisecondorx_newref.R — rwisecondorx_newref(): reference building pipeline. Gender model training → global bin mask → per-partition (/F/M) normalize/PCA/distance-filter/KNN/null-ratios. .build_sub_reference() orchestrates partition. .get_reference() delegates Rcpp. R/rwisecondorx_predict.R — rwisecondorx_predict(): prediction pipeline. Rescale → predict gender → normalize autosomes (coverage + PCA + weights + optimal cutoff + 3-pass within-sample normalization aberration masking) → normalize gonosomes → combine → inflate → log-transform → blacklist → CBS → segment Z-scores → aberration calling. .normalize(), .normalize_repeat(), .normalize_once() implement multi-pass normalization. Global--local index conversion .normalize_once() handles index-space mapping correctly. R/rwisecondorx_cbs.R — .exec_cbs(): CBS wrapper; parallel = TRUE (now default) uses ParDNAcopy::parSegment(num.cores = cpus) explicit thread count hard-stops ParDNAcopy absent; call parallel = FALSE use DNAcopy::segment() explicitly. Matches upstream conventions (0→NA, 0 weights→1e-99, split segments large NA gaps, recalculate weighted means). .get_segment_zscores(): segment-level Z-scores null ratio distributions. R/rwisecondorx_output.R — BED statistics output writers WisecondorXPrediction objects. src/knn_reference.cpp — knn_reference_cpp(): OpenMP-parallelized KNN reference bin finding (leave-one-chromosome-squared Euclidean distance, partial sort K nearest). Stores global 1-based indexes. null_ratios_cpp(): OpenMP-parallelized null ratio computation using global indexes directly full sample vectors. src/nipter_matching.cpp — nipter_ssd_scores_cpp(): one query vs N columns fractions matrix, returns N SSD scores (OpenMP). nipter_ssd_matrix_cpp(): symmetric N×N pairwise SSD matrix (OpenMP, symmetric schedule). accept cpus thread count. src/RcppExports.cpp, R/RcppExports.R — auto-generated Rcpp glue. R/zzz_rcpp.R — @useDynLib RWisecondorX, .registration = TRUE @importFrom Rcpp sourceCpp roxygen directives. src/Makevars, src/Makevars.win — OpenMP compilation flags. Synthetic cohort generator R/synthetic_cohort.R — generate_cohort(): creates synthetic BAMs “compressed” chromosome lengths (100bp per bin) testing. Produces ~435KB BAMs per sample. Trisomy simulation uses Nguyen et al. 2023 fetal fraction model (doi:10.1101/2023.11.24.568620): reads stochastically removed non-target chromosomes probability p = k*f/(1+k*f) simulate relative enrichment trisomy chromosome, k = 0.5 (non-mosaic) k = 0.25 (mosaic). Default fetal fraction 10%. Exports COMPRESSED_BINSIZE constant (100L). inst/scripts/make_cohort.R — CLI wrapper batch cohort generation. Tests — native WisecondorX pipeline inst/tinytest/test_rwisecondorx.R — 76 assertions: reference building (gender model, masking, PCA, KNN indexes distances, null ratios), prediction (normalization, CBS, Z-scores, aberration calling), trisomy detection sensitivity (T21/T18/T13 detected gains, euploid negative control clean). inst/tinytest/test_cohort_pipeline.R — 31 assertions: end--end pipeline (cohort generation → binning → newref → predict → aberration detection). test files use library(RWisecondorX) instead source() hacks. BED.gz reader functions — round-trip stored BED files R/bed_reader.R — bed_to_sample(): reads 4-column BED.gz (bam_convert_bed()) named-list--integer-vectors format rwisecondorx_newref() / rwisecondorx_predict(). bed_to_nipter_sample(): reads 5-column (CombinedStrands) 9-column (SeparatedStrands) BED.gz (nipter_bin_bam_bed()) NIPTeRSample object. Auto-detects column count via read_tabix() probe; handles literal “NA” corrected_count via TRY_CAST. Per-strand corrected values (corrected_fwd, corrected_rev) read independently SeparatedStrands. inst/tinytest/test_bed_reader.R — 46 assertions covering WisecondorX NIPTeR round-trips, SeparatedStrands 9-column BED, corrected per-strand round-trip, sample name inference, scale_sample() integration.

title: RWisecondorX Agent Guidelines
text: NIPTeR layer Sex-stratified NCV X/Y chromosomes: user’s clinical pipeline computes sex-stratified NCV denominators X Y (separate models males vs females). yet implemented. Sex-stratified regression X/Y: Forward stepwise lm() models X Y fractions, stratified predicted sex. yet implemented. Multi-chromosome NIPTeR conformance fixture: bundled inst/extdata/nipter_conformance_fixture.bam regenerated make fixtures / make nipter-fixture. contains 24 chromosomes, unmapped reads, -position collisions, installed-package conformance tests require external BAM. Native WisecondorX layer — remains pipeline (newref + predict) functionally complete 76 unit tests + 31 cohort pipeline tests pass. remaining work validation extension: End--end conformance vs upstream Python: Bin--bin comparison rwisecondorx_newref() + rwisecondorx_predict() output official Python wisecondorx newref + predict real multi-sample BAMs. reference object (PCA components, KNN indexes, null ratios) final aberration calls need match. Requires controlled multi-sample BAM set bioconda wisecondorx package via condathis. yet automated. WisecondorX reference interoperable file: WisecondorXReference object currently serialized RDS file. matrices (PCA components, mask, indexes, distances, null_ratios) dense numeric arrays don’t map naturally flat BED/TSV. structured HDF5 multiple-TSV.bgz serialization allow Python tooling read reference. priority unless user Python consumer. rwisecondorx_newref() multi-file input via tabix_multi: Currently takes list -memory sample lists. accept bed_dir argument using rduckhts_tabix_multi() load WisecondorX 4-column BEDs , analogous nipter_control_group_from_beds(). DONE — rwisecondorx_newref() now accepts bed_dir bed_pattern parameters. Threading budget propagation: rwisecondorx_newref() accepts cpus KNN finding. rwisecondorx_predict() now accepts cpus passes parSegment(). wired together coherently called pipeline script — use cpus value throughout. Beta-mode aberration calling: rwisecondorx_predict() supports zscore beta (purity-based) cutoffs. beta path (ratio > 2^beta) implemented yet tested real data.

what: Known upstream WisecondorX deviations intentionally replicated
title: RWisecondorX Agent Guidelines
text: CPA +1 overcount: upstream Python computes segment length end - start + 1 half-open CBS intervals. rwisecondorx_cbs.R replicates .get_cpa() conformance. Whole-chromosome last-bin drop: upstream Python computes whole-chromosome Z-scores using end = bins_per_chr - 1, drops last bin half-open slicing. rwisecondorx_cbs.R replicates .compute_statistics() conformance.

title: RWisecondorX Agent Guidelines
text: “BED.gz” files BGZF-compressed, tabix-indexed TSV files use BED coordinate conventions (0-based half-open intervals, chr prefix). strict BED files — 3 columns extra columns standard BED fields. Always read read_tabix() (DuckDB SQL) Rduckhts::rduckhts_tabix_multi(), never read.table() BED-aware parsers assume 3-column BED semantics. 4-column WisecondorX TSV.bgz: chrom, start, end, count (written bam_convert_bed()). 5-column NIPTeR TSV.bgz (CombinedStrands): chrom, start, end, count, corrected_count (written nipter_bin_bam_bed()). 9-column NIPTeR TSV.bgz (SeparatedStrands): chrom, start, end, count, count_fwd, count_rev, corrected_count, corrected_fwd, corrected_rev (written nipter_bin_bam_bed(separate_strands = TRUE)). count = count_fwd + count_rev; corrected_* columns NA GC-corrected sample supplied. 5-column GC table TSV.bgz: chrom, start, end, pct_gc, seq_len (written nipter_gc_precompute()). Precompute per reference genome + bin size, pass path nipter_gc_correct(gc_table = ...). files bgzipped (BGZF) tabix-indexed via Rduckhts::rduckhts_bgzip() Rduckhts::rduckhts_tabix_index(). use gzfile() external tools. bed_to_sample() reads 4-column TSV.bgz back WisecondorX -memory format. bed_to_nipter_sample() reads 5- 9-column TSV.bgz back NIPTeRSample. nipter_control_group_from_beds() loads whole directory via rduckhts_tabix_multi() one DuckDB pass. reading uses SQL read_tabix() table function — never read_bed(). WisecondorX reference (WisecondorXReference) still RDS-. matrices (PCA, KNN indexes, null ratios) dense numeric arrays sensibly represented flat TSV. Use saveRDS() / readRDS().

what: Convert Step: Exact SQL Replication of pysam larp/larp2
title: RWisecondorX Agent Guidelines
text: WisecondorX convert step uses streaming dedup state machine (larp/larp2). exact SQL equivalent uses DuckDB window functions FILE_OFFSET: Key subtleties reproduced exactly: Improper pairs (is_paired is_proper) invisible larp larp2. filter internal rmdup = "streaming" — intrinsic WisecondorX algorithm, user flag option. Unpaired reads update larp larp2 — hence IGNORE NULLS trick. larp never reset chromosomes — hence PARTITION . Bin assignment uses integer division pos // binsize matching Python’s int(pos / binsize). pos 0-based: subtract 1 duckhts 1-based POS dividing.
code: LAG(pos) OVER (ORDER BY file_offset)                                          -- prev_pos (larp) LAST_VALUE(CASE WHEN is_paired != 0 THEN pnext END IGNORE NULLS)              -- prev_pnext (larp2)     OVER (ORDER BY file_offset ROWS BETWEEN UNBOUNDED PRECEDING AND 1 PRECEDING)

title: RWisecondorX Agent Guidelines
text: bam_convert(), bam_convert_bed(), nipter_bin_bam(), nipter_bin_bam_bed() expose samtools-style -f/-F flag filtering: require_flags (integer bitmask, default 0L): reads (flag & require_flags) == require_flags kept. Equivalent samtools view -f. exclude_flags (integer bitmask, default 0L): reads (flag & exclude_flags) != 0 dropped. Equivalent samtools view -F. Common real-world NIPT pre-filter pattern (matches samtools view --min-MQ 40 -F 1024):
code: nipter_bin_bam("sample.dm.bam", mapq = 40L, exclude_flags = 1024L)

previous_headings: NIPTeR Layer: Known Issues and Design Decisions
what: NIPTeR’s scanBam approach is “concat and pretend single-ended”
title: RWisecondorX Agent Guidelines
text: NIPTeR’s bin_bam_sample() calls scanBam() without filtering parameters, splits read records strand (+ / -), discarding reads strand "*" (unmapped). applies unique() positions per chromosome per strand sums forward reverse counts single matrix. consequence paired-end data treated concatenated mates single-ended read pool: R1 (forward) R2 (reverse) counted independently respective start positions. paired-end library produces roughly 2× bin counts single-end library sequencing depth, mates every fragment counted. creates fundamental incompatibility: NIPTeR reference (control group) built single-end samples used test paired-end sample, vice versa. practice goes unnoticed samples cohort use library preparation, mixing single-end paired-end data produces wildly incorrect Z-scores NCV values. nipter_bin_bam() replicates behaviour (mates counted) default (rmdup = "none", flag filters), keeping compatible NIPTeR control groups built way. Users need fragment-level counts (one count per fragment, per read) pre-filter R1 using require_flags = 0x41L (paired + read1) count unique fragments — future enhancement.

what: NIPTeR’s implicit positional dedup is similar to WisecondorX streaming dedup, but different
title: RWisecondorX Agent Guidelines
text: NIPTeR’s bin_reads() applies unique() read positions per chromosome per strand binning. effect two reads start position chromosome strand counted , twice. position-based deduplication. WisecondorX’s larp also collapses consecutive reads position. critical differences: collapse -position reads, NIPTeR’s unique() also silently merges two reads happen share start position, including distinct reads PCR duplicates. hg00106_chr11_fixture.bam fixture (1,018 paired-end reads) NIPTeR’s approach count 1,003 vs 1,018 rmdup = "none" — difference 15 reads, positions duplicate flag (0x400) set one mate.

what: Two additional bugs in NIPTeR’s scanBam call
title: RWisecondorX Agent Guidelines
text: Bug 1 — Split length mismatch BAMs unmapped reads. strands[strands != "*"] length N − U (U = unmapped reads) reads length N. R’s split() requires equal lengths, errors silently misbehaves BAM containing unmapped reads. NIPTeR implicitly requires samtools view -F 4 pre-filtering. Bug 2 — Positional dedup via unique() flag-ignorant. tabulate() counts unique positions per bin, reads. PCR duplicate unique position survives; two distinct reads position silently merged. equivalent -F 1024. Consequence conformance testing. Exact bin--bin match NIPTeR::bin_bam_sample() requires BAM unmapped reads two reads sharing start position per strand. package now bundles fixture inst/extdata/nipter_conformance_fixture.bam; NIPTER_CONFORMANCE_BAM override custom fixture constraints. rmdup = "none" closest mode counts read independently rather unique positions.
code: # NIPTeR source — bin_sample.R splitted_reads <- split(x = reads, f = droplevels(strands[strands != "*"])) # NIPTeR source — bin_sample.R / bin_reads() reads <- sapply(X = unique(reads_data_frame[min_read:max_read]),                 FUN = getbins, bin_size = bin_size) bins <- tabulate(reads, nbins = n_bins)

what: KNN Index Semantics — Critical Design Note
title: RWisecondorX Agent Guidelines
text: Rcpp implementation (knn_reference_cpp()) stores global 1-based indexes full masked-bin array. Upstream Python (get_ref_for_bins()) stores local indexes relative chr_data (leave-one-chromosome-subset). consequence: global--local conversion .normalize_once() : - Global index g < chr_start → local g (excised chromosome, unchanged) - Global index g > chr_cum → local g - n_chr (excised chromosome, shifted ) - Global index within [chr_start, chr_cum] never occur (-chromosome bins excluded KNN building) change index storage convention without updating .normalize_once() predict null_ratios_cpp() newref.

title: Background
text: extensively comparing different (shallow) whole-genome sequencing-based copy number detection tools, including WISECONDOR, QDNAseq, CNVkit, Control-FREEC, BIC-seq2 cn.MOPS, WISECONDOR appeared normalize sequencing data consistent way, shown paper. Nevertheless, WISECONDOR limitations: Stouffer’s Z-score approach error-prone dealing large amounts aberrations, algorithm extremely slow (24h) operating small bin sizes (15 kb), sex chromosomes part analysis. , present WisecondorX, evolved WISECONDOR aims dealing previous difficulties, resulting overall superior results significantly lower computing times, allowing daily diagnostic use. WisecondorX meant applicable NIPT, also gDNA, PGT, FFPE, LQB, … etc.

title: Background

title: Background
text: understand underlying algorithm, highly recommend reading Straver et al (2014); update shortly introduced Huijsdens-van Amsterdam et al (2018). Numerous adaptations algorithm made, yet central normalization principles remain. Changes include e.g. inclusion gender prediction algorithm, gender handling prior normalization (ultimately enabling X Y predictions), -sample Z-scoring, inclusion weighted circular binary segmentation algorithm improved codes obtaining tables plots.

title: Background
text: Every dot represents bin. dots range across X-axis chromosome 1 X (Y, case male). vertical position dot represents ratio observed number reads expected number reads, latter ‘normal’ state. values log2-transformed, copy neutral dots close-0. Importantly, notice dots always subject Gaussian noise. Therefore, segments, indicated horizontal white lines, cover bins predicted equal copy number. size dots represents variability reference set. Thus, size increases certainty observation. goes line width segments. Vertical grey bars represent blacklist, matches mostly hypervariable loci repeats. Finally, horizontal colored dotted lines show constitutional 1n 3n states expected (constitutional DNA 100% purity). Often, aberration reach limits, several potential causes: depending type analysis, sample subject tumor fraction, fetal fraction, mosaicism, … etc. Sometimes, segments surpass limits: ’s likely dealing 0n, 4n, 5n, 6n, …

what: An R Port of WisecondorX for Copy Number Analysis using Duckhts
title: An R Port of WisecondorX for Copy Number Analysis using Duckhts
text: RWisecondorX R toolkit copy number analysis trisomy prediction non-invasive prenatal testing (NIPT), built top Rduckhts DuckDB. covers two upstream method families: WisecondorX CNV detection NIPTeR chromosomal-fraction based trisomy prediction package structured around three workflows: Native R WisecondorX: bam_convert(), bam_convert_bed(), rwisecondorx_newref(), rwisecondorx_predict() Native R NIPTeR: nipter_bin_bam(), GC correction, chi correction, Z-score, NCV, regression, sex prediction, reference QC, plotting Optional upstream conformance: bam_convert_npz() plus wisecondorx_convert(), wisecondorx_newref(), wisecondorx_predict() native convert step backed bam_bin_counts(...) kernel Rduckhts. reproduces upstream WisecondorX larp / larp2 streaming deduplication behaviour, keeping core processing inside R DuckDB.

title: An R Port of WisecondorX for Copy Number Analysis using Duckhts
text: RWisecondorX developed current edge Rduckhts, older CRAN snapshot. Install Rduckhts r-universe first, install RWisecondorX: RWisecondorX imports Rduckhts, DBI, duckdb. reticulate needed write .npz files. condathis needed upstream CLI wrappers.
code: install.packages(   "Rduckhts",   repos = c(     "https://rgenomicsetl.r-universe.dev",     "https://cloud.r-project.org"   ) )  # install.packages("pak") pak::pak("sounkou-bioinfo/RWisecondorX")

title: An R Port of WisecondorX for Copy Number Analysis using Duckhts

title: An R Port of WisecondorX for Copy Number Analysis using Duckhts
text: rmdup argument controls duplicate handling: "streaming" reproduces upstream WisecondorX larp / larp2 "none" skips deduplication entirely "flag" respects SAM duplicate flag (0x400)
code: bins <- bam_convert(   "sample.dm.bam",   binsize = 100000L,   mapq = 1L,   rmdup = "streaming" )

title: An R Port of WisecondorX for Copy Number Analysis using Duckhts
text: bam_convert_bed() writes bgzipped, tabix-indexed BED-like TSV columns chrom, start, end, count. main file format used native R WisecondorX workflow. NIPTeR side, bed_to_nipter_sample() reads 5-column 9-column BED.gz back NIPTeRSample object.
code: bed_file <- "sample.wisecondorx.bed.gz"  bam_convert_bed(   bam     = "sample.dm.bam",   bed     = bed_file,   binsize = 100000L,   rmdup   = "streaming" )  sample_reloaded <- bed_to_sample(bed_file, binsize = 100000L)

title: An R Port of WisecondorX for Copy Number Analysis using Duckhts
text: rwisecondorx_newref() rwisecondorx_predict() pure R/Rcpp implementations reference-building prediction stages. main production path want WisecondorX-style CNV calling without Python runtime dependency.
code: control_bams <- c("ctrl_01.dm.bam", "ctrl_02.dm.bam", "ctrl_03.dm.bam") control_samples <- lapply(   control_bams,   function(bam) bam_convert(bam, binsize = 100000L, mapq = 1L, rmdup = "streaming") ) names(control_samples) <- sub("\\.bam$", "", basename(control_bams))  ref <- rwisecondorx_newref(   samples = control_samples,   binsize = 100000L,   nipt = TRUE,   cpus = 4L )  test_sample <- bam_convert("test.dm.bam", binsize = 100000L, mapq = 1L, rmdup = "streaming") pred <- rwisecondorx_predict(test_sample, ref, zscore = 5, seed = 42L, cpus = 4L) pred$aberrations

title: An R Port of WisecondorX for Copy Number Analysis using Duckhts
text: native predictor exposes controls commonly needed practice: beta purity-based ratio calling gender = "F" / "M" override Y-fraction classifier parallel = TRUE ParDNAcopy, parallel = FALSE serial DNAcopy::segment()
code: pred_forced <- rwisecondorx_predict(   test_sample,   ref,   gender = "F",   beta = NULL,   seed = 42L )

title: An R Port of WisecondorX for Copy Number Analysis using Duckhts
text: write_wisecondorx_output() writes: <outprefix>_bins.bed <outprefix>_segments.bed <outprefix>_aberrations.bed <outprefix>_statistics.txt rwisecondorx_ref_qc() computes upstream-style reference QC heuristics returns structured report, optional JSON output. package yet provide native ggplot helpers WisecondorXPrediction objects. need upstream PNG/PDF plotting behaviour, use wisecondorx_predict(..., plot = TRUE) optional CLI wrapper path.
code: qc <- rwisecondorx_ref_qc(ref, output_json = "reference_qc.json") write_wisecondorx_output(pred, outprefix = "results/sample_01")

title: An R Port of WisecondorX for Copy Number Analysis using Duckhts
text: rwisecondorx_predict() supports beta parameter purity-based aberration calling: beta supplied, ratio cutoffs replace Z-score thresholds: mode somatic CNV analysis, routine NIPT trisomy calling.
code: pred_somatic <- rwisecondorx_predict(   sample    = tumour_sample,   reference = ref,   beta      = 0.4,   seed      = 1L ) gain cutoff: log2((ploidy + beta/2) / ploidy) loss cutoff: log2((ploidy - beta/2) / ploidy)

title: An R Port of WisecondorX for Copy Number Analysis using Duckhts
text: native NIPTeR layer covers binning, control-group construction, GC correction, chi correction, autosomal scoring, sex prediction, sex-chromosome models, reference QC plotting.

title: An R Port of WisecondorX for Copy Number Analysis using Duckhts
text: nipter_bin_bam() produces NIPTeRSample objects compatible downstream NIPTeR-style functions.
code: samples <- lapply(bam_files, nipter_bin_bam, binsize = 50000L)

title: An R Port of WisecondorX for Copy Number Analysis using Duckhts
text: nipter_as_control_group() validates deduplicates reference cohort. nipter_diagnose_control_group() reports chromosome-level diagnostics. nipter_match_control_group() nipter_match_matrix() support sample matching outlier QC.
code: cg <- nipter_as_control_group(samples) diag <- nipter_diagnose_control_group(cg) matched_cg <- nipter_match_control_group(test_sample, cg, n = 50L) mm <- nipter_match_matrix(cg, cpus = 4L)

title: An R Port of WisecondorX for Copy Number Analysis using Duckhts
text: nipter_gc_correct() adjusts bin counts GC-content bias. repeated workflows, nipter_gc_precompute() stores GC table TSV.bgz file.
code: nipter_gc_precompute(   fasta = "hg38.fa",   binsize = 50000L,   out = "gc_table.tsv.bgz" )  cg <- nipter_gc_correct(cg, gc_table = "gc_table.tsv.bgz") test_sample <- nipter_gc_correct(test_sample, gc_table = "gc_table.tsv.bgz")

title: An R Port of WisecondorX for Copy Number Analysis using Duckhts
text: nipter_chi_correct() identifies overdispersed bins across control group applies correction sample control cohort.
code: corrected   <- nipter_chi_correct(test_sample, cg, chi_cutoff = 3.5) test_sample <- corrected$sample cg          <- corrected$control_group

title: An R Port of WisecondorX for Copy Number Analysis using Duckhts
text: nipter_z_score() computes chromosomal fraction Z-scores: nipter_ncv_score() computes normalized chromosome values: nipter_regression() uses forward stepwise regression:
code: z21 <- nipter_z_score(test_sample, cg, chromo_focus = 21) z21$sample_z_score ncv21 <- nipter_ncv_score(test_sample, cg, chromo_focus = 21) ncv21$sample_ncv_score ncv21$denominators reg21 <- nipter_regression(test_sample, cg, chromo_focus = 21) reg21$models[[1]]$z_score reg21$models[[1]]$predictors

title: An R Port of WisecondorX for Copy Number Analysis using Duckhts
text: production use, package exposes typed reference-building QC layer: nipter_build_reference() packages control group, sex models, reference frame nipter_build_gaunosome_models() adds sex-stratified X/Y NCV regression models nipter_control_group_qc() computes chromosome-, sample-, sex-model-, optional bin-level QC summaries write_nipter_reference_plots() writes current plot bundle current plotting helpers : nipter_plot_qc_chromosomes() nipter_plot_qc_samples() nipter_plot_qc_bins(..., metric = "cv_scaled" | "chi_z") nipter_plot_reference_sex_boxplots() nipter_plot_reference_sex_scatter(..., space = "fraction" |     "ratio")
code: reference <- nipter_build_reference(   cg,   sample_sex = sample_sex,   y_unique_ratios = y_unique_ratios ) reference <- nipter_build_gaunosome_models(reference)  qc <- nipter_control_group_qc(   reference$control_group,   reference_model = reference,   include_bins = TRUE )  plot_paths <- write_nipter_reference_plots(   qc,   reference,   outprefix = "nipter_reference_qc" )

title: An R Port of WisecondorX for Copy Number Analysis using Duckhts
text: nipter_sex_model() trains Gaussian-mixture models sex prediction Y-fraction, X/Y fraction pairs, Y-unique ratios. nipter_predict_sex() combines one models majority vote.
code: cg <- nipter_as_control_group(samples) cg <- nipter_gc_correct(cg, fasta = "hg38.fa") corrected <- nipter_chi_correct(samples[[1]], cg) cg <- corrected$control_group  model_y  <- nipter_sex_model(cg, method = "y_fraction") model_xy <- nipter_sex_model(cg, method = "xy_fraction")  nipter_predict_sex(test_sample, models = list(model_y, model_xy))

title: Count reads per bin from a BAM or CRAM file — bam_convert
code: bam_convert(   bam,   binsize = 5000L,   mapq = 1L,   require_flags = 0L,   exclude_flags = 0L,   rmdup = c("streaming", "flag", "none"),   separate_strands = FALSE,   con = NULL,   reference = NULL )

title: Count reads per bin from a BAM or CRAM file — bam_convert
text: bam Path indexed BAM CRAM file. binsize Bin size base pairs. Default 5000L (WisecondorX); use 50000L NIPTeR-style workflows. mapq Minimum mapping quality. Default 1L (WisecondorX / samtools default). Set 0L retain reads regardless MAPQ (NIPTeR). require_flags Integer bitmask. reads (FLAG & require_flags) == require_flags retained. 0L (default) imposes requirement. Example: require_flags = 0x2L keeps properly paired reads. exclude_flags Integer bitmask. Reads (FLAG & exclude_flags) != 0 dropped. 0L (default) drops nothing. Example: exclude_flags = 0xF04L drops unmapped, secondary, QC-fail supplementary reads (common samtools pre-filter). rmdup Duplicate removal strategy. "streaming" (default) applies WisecondorX larp/larp2 algorithm (also excludes improper pairs). "flag" drops reads SAM flag 0x400. "none" keeps reads pass filters. separate_strands Logical; TRUE, returns per-strand counts (forward + reverse -). return value changes list two named lists (fwd rev), structured like default return. Used nipter_bin_bam(separate_strands = TRUE) NIPTeR SeparatedStrands object. Default FALSE. con Optional open DBI connection duckhts already loaded. NULL (default) temporary -memory DuckDB connection created. reference Optional FASTA reference path CRAM inputs.

title: Count reads per bin from a BAM or CRAM file — bam_convert
text: separate_strands = FALSE (default): list-like WisecondorXSample S7 object one integer vector per chromosome key ("1"–"22", "23" X, "24" Y). vector contains per-bin read counts (bin 0 = positions 0 binsize - 1). Chromosomes present BAM header returned dense vectors padded trailing zeros chromosome span implied header binsize; chromosomes absent header NULL. separate_strands = TRUE: list two elements, fwd rev, structured like default return.

title: Count reads per bin from a BAM or CRAM file — bam_convert
text: Read filtering mirrors samtools view convention: mapq sets minimum mapping quality; require_flags bitmask flags must set (equivalent samtools view -f); exclude_flags bitmask flags must clear (equivalent samtools view -F).  Use duckhts UDF sam_flag_bits(flag) inspect named flag fields, sam_flag_has(flag, bit) test individual bits. rmdup controls duplicate removal independently flag filters: "streaming" applies WisecondorX larp/larp2 consecutive-position state machine also enforces WisecondorX improper-pair rule (paired reads properly paired excluded counting dedup state — intrinsic algorithm, flag option); "flag" additionally excludes reads SAM flag 0x400 set (Picard / sambamba pre-marked duplicates); "none" applies deduplication. heavy lifting delegated native bam_bin_counts(...) kernel bundled Rduckhts; RWisecondorX reshapes fixed-bin output chromosome-keyed objects expected WisecondorX NIPTeR layers.

title: Count reads per bin from a BAM or CRAM file — bam_convert
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # WisecondorX defaults bins <- bam_convert("sample.bam")  # NIPTeR defaults — all mapped reads, no dedup, 50 kb bins bins <- bam_convert("sample.bam", binsize = 50000L, mapq = 0L,                     rmdup = "none")  # Pre-filtered BAM: skip unmapped + secondary + supplementary, flag dedup bins <- bam_convert("sample.bam",                     exclude_flags = bitwOr(0x4L, bitwOr(0x100L, 0x800L)),                     rmdup = "flag") } # }

title: Convert BAM/CRAM to a bgzipped BED bin-count file — bam_convert_bed
code: bam_convert_bed(   bam,   bed,   binsize = 5000L,   mapq = 1L,   require_flags = 0L,   exclude_flags = 0L,   rmdup = c("streaming", "flag", "none"),   con = NULL,   reference = NULL,   index = TRUE,   metadata = NULL )

title: Convert BAM/CRAM to a bgzipped BED bin-count file — bam_convert_bed
text: bam Path indexed BAM CRAM file. bed Path output .bed.gz file (created overwritten). tabix index written paste0(bed, ".tbi"). binsize Bin size base pairs (default 5000). mapq Minimum mapping quality (default 1L). require_flags Integer bitmask; reads bits set kept (samtools -f). Default 0L (requirement). exclude_flags Integer bitmask; reads bit set dropped (samtools -F). Default 0L (nothing dropped). rmdup Duplicate-removal strategy passed bam_convert(). con Optional open DBI connection duckhts already loaded. NULL (default), temporary -memory DuckDB connection created. reference Optional FASTA reference path CRAM inputs. index Logical; TRUE (default) tabix index created alongside bgzipped output. metadata Optional named list named atomic vector provenance metadata write leading ##RWX_<key>=<value> lines BED body. data rows remain headerless. Default NULL writes metadata.

Passed bam_convert(). np Optional numpy module imported via reticulate::import("numpy"). NULL (default) imported automatically. reference Optional FASTA reference path CRAM inputs. Passed bam_convert().

title: Convert BAM/CRAM to WisecondorX NPZ format — bam_convert_npz
text: function exists Python WisecondorX CLI conformance interoperability. native R pipeline uses bam_convert_bed() together rwisecondorx_newref() rwisecondorx_predict(). resulting NPZ three top-level keys: sample 0-d object array wrapping dict mapping "1".."24" int32 arrays (chromosome bin counts). binsize Scalar int (bin size bp). quality 0-d object array wrapping dict QC counters (populated zeros since native binning kernel track per-read filter stats). writer's numpy >= 2.0 internal pickle references numpy._core, older numpy (< 2.0) unpickle.  function patches pickle bytestream use numpy.core NPZ readable numpy 1.x 2.x. Native bam_convert() returns dense vectors covering chromosome span BAM header. Upstream wisecondorx convert one additional historical quirk: allocates int(length / binsize + 1) bins every chromosome header, chromosomes whose lengths exact multiples binsize carry one extra trailing -zero bin. function pads upstream NPZ layout serialisation Python wisecondorx newref/predict remains byte-compatible, native R list/BED paths keep cleaner header-span contract.

title: Read a NIPTeR-format BED file into a NIPTeRSample — bed_to_nipter_sample
text: bed Path bgzipped (plain) BED file .tbi index. name Optional sample name. NULL (default), derived BED file basename (e.g. "sample" "sample.bed.gz"). binsize Optional integer; bin size base pairs. NULL (default), inferred first row BED file. autosomal_source autosomal counts realize returned sample. "auto" (default) uses corrected autosomal columns present, otherwise raw counts. "raw" always uses raw count columns. "corrected" requires corrected columns present. sex_source sex-chromosome counts realize returned sample. "match" (default) follows autosomal_source. "raw" always uses raw count columns. "corrected" requires corrected sex columns present. con Optional open DBI connection duckhts already loaded.

Optional leading ##RWX_<key>=<value> metadata lines ignored automatically. number columns detected automatically. corrected columns contain non-NA values (.e. BED written GC-corrected corrected argument), autosomal_source = "auto" (default) realizes corrected autosomal counts sex_source = "match" follows choice X/Y. Callers can override request raw corrected sex-chromosome values independently autosomes. SeparatedStrands, per-strand corrected values (corrected_fwd, corrected_rev) used corrected sex counts selected.

what: Bin a BAM/CRAM file — NIPTeR style — nipter_bin_bam
title: Bin a BAM/CRAM file — NIPTeR style — nipter_bin_bam
text: Replicates NIPTeR's bin_bam_sample(): counts reads fixed-width bins across chromosomes 1-22, X, Y. original NIPTeR counts mapped reads MAPQ filter; real-world NIPT pipelines typically pre-filter samtools view --min-MQ 40 -F 1024 binning. modes supported mapq, exclude_flags, require_flags parameters.

title: Bin a BAM/CRAM file — NIPTeR style — nipter_bin_bam
code: nipter_bin_bam(   bam,   binsize = 50000L,   mapq = 0L,   require_flags = 0L,   exclude_flags = 0L,   rmdup = c("none", "flag"),   separate_strands = FALSE,   con = NULL,   reference = NULL )

title: Bin a BAM/CRAM file — NIPTeR style — nipter_bin_bam
text: bam Path indexed BAM CRAM file. binsize Bin size base pairs. Default 50000 (NIPTeR's fixed bin size). Must match binsize used building control group. mapq Minimum mapping quality. Default 0L (NIPTeR original: mapped reads). Set 40L match common NIPT pipeline pre-filtering (samtools view --min-MQ 40). require_flags Integer bitmask; reads bits set kept (samtools -f). Default 0L (requirement). exclude_flags Integer bitmask; reads bit set dropped (samtools -F). Default 0L. Set 1024L (0x400) exclude reads marked duplicates (samtools view -F 1024). rmdup Duplicate removal strategy. "none" (default, NIPTeR standard) "flag" (equivalent exclude_flags = 1024L; BAMs already processed Picard / sambamba). NIPTeR perform streaming deduplication. separate_strands Logical; TRUE, produces SeparatedStrands object independent forward/reverse count matrices. Default FALSE (CombinedStrands). con Optional open DBI connection duckhts already loaded. reference Optional FASTA reference path CRAM inputs.

title: Bin a BAM/CRAM file — NIPTeR style — nipter_bin_bam
text: object class c("NIPTeRSample", <strand_type>): CombinedStrands (default): autosomal_chromosome_reads list one 22-row integer matrix (rows "1"–"22"); sex_chromosome_reads list one 2-row matrix (rows "X", "Y"). SeparatedStrands (separate_strands = TRUE): autosomal_chromosome_reads list two matrices — element 1 forward (rows "1F"–"22F"), element 2 reverse (rows "1R"–"22R"); sex_chromosome_reads similarly contains forward ("XF", "YF") reverse ("XR", "YR") matrices.

title: Bin a BAM/CRAM file — NIPTeR style — nipter_bin_bam
text: result reshaped NIPTeRSample object whose structure parallels NIPTeR's NIPTSample: autosomal reads chromosome--bin matrix sex chromosome reads separate two-row matrix. separate_strands = TRUE, forward (+) reverse (-) reads counted independently, producing two matrices per chromosome set (class "SeparatedStrands"). doubles predictor pool nipter_regression() — see NIPTeR documentation details.

title: Bin a BAM/CRAM file — NIPTeR style — nipter_bin_bam
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # NIPTeR original defaults: all mapped reads, no dedup sample <- nipter_bin_bam("sample.bam", binsize = 50000L)  # Common NIPT pipeline: MAPQ >= 40, exclude duplicate-flagged reads sample <- nipter_bin_bam("sample.dm.bam", binsize = 50000L,                          mapq = 40L, exclude_flags = 1024L)  # SeparatedStrands for regression with doubled predictor pool sample_ss <- nipter_bin_bam("sample.bam", separate_strands = TRUE)  sample$autosomal_chromosome_reads[[1]]["21", ]   # chr21 bin counts } # }

text: bam Path indexed BAM CRAM file. bed Path output .bed.gz file. tabix index written alongside paste0(bed, ".tbi"). binsize Bin size base pairs (default 50000). mapq Minimum mapping quality (default 0L). Set 40L match common NIPT pre-filtering (samtools view --min-MQ 40). require_flags Integer bitmask; reads bits set kept (samtools -f). Default 0L. exclude_flags Integer bitmask; reads bit set dropped (samtools -F). Default 0L. Set 1024L exclude duplicate-flagged reads (samtools view -F 1024). rmdup Duplicate removal strategy: "none" (default) "flag". separate_strands Logical; TRUE, includes separate count_fwd count_rev columns. Default FALSE. con Optional open DBI connection duckhts already loaded. reference Optional FASTA reference path CRAM inputs. index Logical; create tabix index (default TRUE). metadata Optional named list named atomic vector provenance metadata write leading ##RWX_<key>=<value> lines BED body. data rows remain headerless. Default NULL writes metadata.

what: Chi-squared correction for overdispersed bins — nipter_chi_correct
title: Chi-squared correction for overdispersed bins — nipter_chi_correct
text: Identifies bins control group excess variance (overdispersion) using chi-squared test downweights . reduces influence noisy bins downstream Z-scores NCV calculations.

title: Chi-squared correction for overdispersed bins — nipter_chi_correct
code: nipter_chi_correct(   sample,   control_group,   chi_cutoff = 3.5,   include_sex = FALSE )

title: Chi-squared correction for overdispersed bins — nipter_chi_correct
text: sample NIPTeRSample object (test sample). control_group NIPTeRControlGroup object. chi_cutoff Normalised chi-squared threshold. Bins (chi - df) / sqrt(2*df) > chi_cutoff corrected. Default 3.5. include_sex Logical; correct sex chromosomes well? Default FALSE. Sex chromosome bins use chi-squared weights derived autosomes.

title: Chi-squared correction for overdispersed bins — nipter_chi_correct
text: list two elements: sample corrected NIPTeRSample. control_group corrected NIPTeRControlGroup.

title: Chi-squared correction for overdispersed bins — nipter_chi_correct
text: correction applied simultaneously test sample control group samples, maintaining consistency. algorithm follows NIPTeR's chi-squared correction: control sample, scale autosomal bin counts total reads match overall mean across control samples. Compute expected count per bin (mean scaled counts). Compute chi-squared per bin: \(\chi^2 = \sum_i (expected - scaled_i)^2 / expected\). Normalise: \(z = (\chi^2 - df) / \sqrt{2 \cdot df}\) \(df = n_{controls} - 1\). bins \(z > \text{chi\_cutoff}\): divide reads \(\chi^2 / df\).

title: Chi-squared correction for overdispersed bins — nipter_chi_correct

text: Summarises stability NIPTControlGroup chromosome, sample, optional autosomal-bin level. chromosome summary exposes mean, standard deviation, coefficient variation, Shapiro-Wilk normality statistic autosome, across multiple scoring spaces one long table: post-chi chromosomal fractions, NCV, four RBZ predictor sets. sample summary adds SSD-based matching scores plus strand-aware chromosomal alert counts post-chi diagnostic pass. sex labels available, sample summary enriched per-sample sex-cluster metrics written typed reference frame (ConsensusGender, sex-outlier flag, Z_X_XX/Z_X_XY/ Z_Y_XX/Z_Y_XY), report also includes sex-stratified RR_X/RR_Y spread metrics relevant gaunosome model readiness plus dedicated long-form sex-model summary XX/XY sex-chromosome Z, NCV, regression models. title: Build a typed QC report for a NIPTeR control group — nipter_control_group_qc
text: control_group NIPTControlGroup. sample_sex Optional character vector overriding sex labels stored control_group. Accepted values "female", "male", "ambiguous", "unknown". reference_model Optional NIPTReferenceModel built controls. supplied, QC bundle also extracts explicit XX/XY sex-chromosome Z, NCV, regression model summaries . chi_cutoff Numeric scalar used flag overdispersed bins optional chi profile. Default 3.5. z_cutoff Absolute post-chi z-score threshold used counting aberrant chromosome rows sample summary. Default 3. collapse_strands Logical; FALSE (default), separated strands retain legacy F/R-specific diagnostic rows sample summary reports distinct chromosomes bidirectional aberrations. TRUE, sample-level aberration counts collapse 22 autosomes. max_aberrant_chromosomes Maximum number distinct aberrant chromosomes allowed is_chromosomal_outlier set sample summary. Default 2L. outlier_rule Character scalar describing convert abberant_scores sample-level outlier flags. "any_aberrant_score" (default) removes sample least one post-chi chromosome row beyond z_cutoff. "bidirectional_or_multichromosome" drops sample strands one chromosome aberrant many distinct chromosomes aberrant. include_bins Logical; TRUE, compute autosomal bin-level scaled-count CV chi profile. Default FALSE. rbz_train_fraction Fraction controls used fit autosomal RBZ QC models. Values 1 use train/stat split; values greater equal 1 fit score retained controls. Default 1. rbz_seed Integer seed used rbz_train_fraction < 1. Default 1995L. rbz_exclude_chromosomes Integer vector excluded autosomal RBZ predictor search. Default c(13L, 18L, 21L).

title: GC-correct a NIPTeR sample or control group — nipter_gc_correct
text: object NIPTeRSample NIPTeRControlGroup. fasta Path indexed reference FASTA file (.fa/.fasta .fai index). Ignored gc_table supplied. method GC correction method: "loess" (default) "bin" (bin-weight). span LOESS smoothing parameter (used method = "loess"). Default 0.75. include_sex Logical; correct sex chromosomes (X, Y) well? Default FALSE. binsize Bin size used binning sample (default 50000L). Must match binsize sample. Ignored gc_table list (bin size already encoded vector lengths). gc_table Pre-computed GC table. Either path TSV.bgz file (nipter_gc_precompute) -memory named list returned previous .get_gc_table() call. NULL (default), GC content computed fasta. con Optional open DBI connection duckhts loaded. NULL, temporary connection created.

title: GC-correct a NIPTeR sample or control group — nipter_gc_correct
text: corrected copy object class. Correction status updated "Uncorrected" "GC Corrected".

title: GC-correct a NIPTeR sample or control group — nipter_gc_correct
text: GC content can supplied three ways via gc_table parameter: Pre-computed file Path TSV.bgz produced nipter_gc_precompute. Fastest large cohorts — compute , reuse every sample. -memory list Named list numeric vectors (one per chromosome) returned .get_gc_table(). Useful chaining corrections within session. FASTA path via fasta Compute GC --fly every call. Convenient single-sample use; slow many samples. LOESS method (default): Fits LOESS curve read counts vs GC percentage across autosomal bins known GC non-zero reads. bin scaled median(counts) / fitted(loess), bins normalised genome-wide median. NIPTeR default method. Bin-weight method: Groups bins GC percentage (0.1\ computes mean read count per GC bucket, scales bin global_mean / bucket_mean. Faster LOESS less smooth. Sex chromosome correction (include_sex = TRUE) uses nearest-neighbour lookup autosomal LOESS curve (LOESS method) GC bucket weights (bin-weight method).

title: Regression-based Z-score for trisomy prediction — nipter_regression
text: sample NIPTeRSample object. control_group NIPTeRControlGroup object. chromo_focus Integer; target chromosome (1-22). n_models Number regression models build (default 4). n_predictors Maximum number predictor chromosomes per model (default 4). exclude_chromosomes Integer vector chromosomes exclude predictor pool (default c(13, 18, 21)). include_chromosomes Integer vector chromosomes force-include. train_fraction Fraction control samples used training. Values 1 perform train/stat split; values greater equal 1 fit score controls. Default 0.6. overdispersion_rate Overdispersion multiplier theoretical CV (default 1.15). force_practical_cv Logical; always use practical CV regardless theoretical CV? Default FALSE. seed Random seed train/test split. Default NULL (seed set).

title: Regression-based Z-score for trisomy prediction — nipter_regression
text: list class "NIPTeRRegression" elements: models list n_models sublists, containing: z_score (numeric), cv (selected CV), cv_type ("practical" "theoretical"), predictors (character vector predictor chromosomes), shapiro_p_value, control_z_scores (named numeric vector). focus_chromosome Character. correction_status Character vector. sample_name Character.

title: Regression-based Z-score for trisomy prediction — nipter_regression
text: SeparatedStrands samples predictor pool doubled (autosomal chromosome contributes forward reverse fraction), complementary-strand exclusion within model prevents selecting strands chromosome predictors model. Across models, exact predictor string excluded; complementary strand remains available. algorithm: Split control group train statistic sets according train_fraction. train_fraction >= 1, controls used fitting statistic calculation. Compute chromosomal fractions samples. n_models models, greedily select n_predictors chromosomes maximising adjusted R-squared (forward stepwise selection). Predictors already used previous models excluded. model, fit lm(focus ~ predictors) training set, predict test set, compute: Practical CV: sd(obs/pred) / mean(obs/pred) test set. Theoretical CV: overdispersion_rate / sqrt(total_reads_focus) test sample. larger CV used (unless force_practical_cv = TRUE). Sample Z-score: (sample_ratio - 1) / cv.

title: Build a sex prediction model from a NIPTeR control group — nipter_sex_model
text: control_group NIPTeRControlGroup object. Ideally chi-squared corrected via nipter_chi_correct. method Character; feature space GMM: "y_fraction" Univariate: Y-chromosome read fraction relative total autosomal reads. "xy_fraction" Bivariate: X Y chromosome fractions.

title: Build a sex prediction model from a NIPTeR control group — nipter_sex_model
text: list-like NIPTeRSexModel S7 object elements: model mclust::Mclust fitted object. method Character; method used. male_cluster Integer (1 2); cluster male (higher Y fraction). classifications Named character vector "male"/ "female" labels control sample. fractions Matrix input features used fitting (samples rows). Columns "y_fraction" c("x_fraction", "y_fraction").

title: Build a sex prediction model from a NIPTeR control group — nipter_sex_model
text: mirrors approach used clinical NIPT pipelines (see Details), ported R using mclust::Mclust() place Python's sklearn.GaussianMixture. algorithm: Compute sex chromosome read fractions every sample control group. Y fraction = sum(Y bins) / sum(autosomal bins); X fraction = sum(X bins) / sum(autosomal bins). Fit two-component Gaussian mixture equal mixing proportions (mclust::Mclust(data, G = 2,     control = mclust::emControl(equalPro = TRUE))). Identify male cluster component higher median Y fraction. follows clinical NIPT pipeline pattern building sex models control cohorts. user's pipeline builds three models (Y-unique ratio samtools, XY fractions, Y fraction) takes majority vote; nipter_predict_sex() implements consensus given multiple models.

title: Build a sex prediction model from a NIPTeR control group — nipter_sex_model
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Build sex model from chi-corrected control group sex_model <- nipter_sex_model(chi_cg, method = "y_fraction")  # Bivariate X+Y model sex_model_xy <- nipter_sex_model(chi_cg, method = "xy_fraction") } # }

text: GRCh37, default interval set bundled legacy extdata/grch37_Y_chrom_blacklist.bed, matches historical samtools stats -t human_g1k_v37.fasta_Y_chrom_blacklist.bed path used production shell pipeline. smaller 7-gene interval file can still supplied explicitly regions_file, longer default reproduce legacy YUniqueRatioFiltered semantics. Interval chromosome names matched BAM header Y chrY-style contigs work. Y-target BED whole-nuclear BED apply MAPQ flag filters. Y-target reads summed across supplied intervals exactly listed regions_file. intentionally preserves historical interval-wise summation semantics: two target intervals overlap, read contributing intervals counted twice.

text: samples List sample objects, named list integer vectors keyed chromosome ("1"–"24"), returned bam_convert(). least 10 samples required. Mutually exclusive bed_dir. binsize Integer; target bin size base pairs. samples rescaled size. Default 100000L. Inferred BED files bed_dir supplied sample_binsizes NULL. sample_binsizes Optional integer vector per-sample bin sizes. NULL (default), samples assumed already binsize. nipt Logical; TRUE, NIPT mode (gender correction, male gonosomal reference). Default FALSE. refsize Integer; number reference bin locations per target bin. Default 300L. yfrac Optional numeric; manual Y-fraction cutoff gender classification. NULL (default), cutoff derived GMM. gender_model_names Character scalar; mclust covariance model name used Y-fraction Gaussian mixture gender-model fitting. Default "V", matching current upstream WisecondorX implementation. female_partition_min_samples Integer; minimum female samples required applying female-specific masking building female gonosomal partition. NIPT mode also minimum female count required proceed. Default 5L. male_partition_min_samples Integer; minimum male samples required applying male-specific masking building male gonosomal partition non-NIPT mode. Default 5L. mask_min_median_coverage_fraction Numeric scalar; bins summed normalized coverage fraction global median masked model fitting. Default 0.05. gender_model_grid_min Numeric scalar; lower bound density grid used locate Y-fraction cutoff yfrac provided. Default 0. gender_model_grid_max Numeric scalar; upper bound density grid. Default 0.02. gender_model_grid_length Integer; number grid points used locate Y-fraction cutoff. Default 5000L. pca_components Integer; PCA components retained -sample normalization. Default 5L. pca_distance_min_class_bins Integer; minimum masked bins required applying class-specific PCA-distance pruning. Default 10L. pca_distance_autosome_mad_multiplier Numeric; MAD multiplier used autosomal PCA-distance pruning. Default 20. pca_distance_autosome_floor Numeric; minimum autosomal PCA-distance cutoff. Default 10. pca_distance_chrX_mad_multiplier Numeric; MAD multiplier used chrX PCA-distance pruning. Default 20. pca_distance_chrX_floor Numeric; minimum chrX PCA-distance cutoff. Default 10. pca_distance_chrY_mad_multiplier Numeric; MAD multiplier used chrY PCA-distance pruning. Default 50. pca_distance_chrY_floor Numeric; minimum chrY PCA-distance cutoff. Default 15. null_ratio_max_samples Integer; maximum number cohort samples used sampling columns null-ratio estimation. Default 100L, matching current upstream implementation. cpus Integer; number threads reference bin finding. Default 4L. bed_dir Optional character; path directory 4-column bgzipped BED files (written bam_convert_bed()). files matching bed_pattern loaded single DuckDB pass via rduckhts_tabix_multi(). Mutually exclusive samples. bed_pattern Glob pattern matching BED files inside bed_dir. Default "*.bed.gz". con Optional existing DuckDB connection. Used bed_dir supplied. temporary connection created (closed) NULL.

title: Build a WisecondorX reference from binned samples — rwisecondorx_newref
text: pipeline trains gender model (2-component GMM Y-fractions), optionally applies gender correction non-NIPT workflows, computes global bin mask, builds three sub-references: autosomes (), female gonosomes (F), male gonosomes (M). sub-reference includes PCA components, within-sample reference bin indices distances, null ratios -sample Z-scoring. faithful port upstream Python wisecondorx newref, crediting original WisecondorX authors. returned reference object contains: binsize Integer; reference bin size. is_nipt Logical; whether NIPT mode used. trained_cutoff Numeric; Y-fraction gender cutoff. has_female Logical; whether female gonosomal reference exists. has_male Logical; whether male gonosomal reference exists. algorithm_params Named list native reference-building parameters used create object. mask, bins_per_chr, masked_bins_per_chr, masked_bins_per_chr_cum, pca_components, pca_mean, indexes, distances, null_ratios Autosomal reference components. mask.F, ..., null_ratios.F Female gonosomal reference (present). mask.M, ..., null_ratios.M Male gonosomal reference (present).

Used rescaling reference bin size. outprefix Character; path prefix output files. BED statistics files written <outprefix>_bins.bed, <outprefix>_segments.bed, <outprefix>_aberrations.bed, <outprefix>_statistics.txt. NULL (default), files written results returned list. minrefbins Integer; minimum reference bins per target. Bins fewer references zeroed . Default 150L. maskrepeats Integer; number iterative distance-masking cycles. Default 5L. alpha Numeric; CBS breakpoint p-value threshold. Default 1e-4. zscore Numeric; Z-score cutoff aberration calling. Default 5. optimal_cutoff_sd_multiplier Numeric; number population standard deviations added mean distance iteratively deriving optimal within-reference cutoff. Default 3. within_sample_mask_iterations Integer; number iterative within-sample masking passes. Default 3L. within_sample_mask_quantile Numeric scalar (0, 1); bins |z| >= qnorm(within_sample_mask_quantile) excluded next within-sample normalization pass. Default 0.99. cbs_split_min_gap_bp Integer; NA stretches spanning many base pairs trigger CBS segment splitting. Default 2000000L. segment_zscore_cap Numeric; absolute cap applied segment z-scores derived null-ratio distributions. Default 1000. beta Optional numeric; given, ratio-based cutoff used instead Z-score. approximate purity (0, 1]. Default NULL. blacklist Optional character; path headerless BED file regions mask. gender Optional character; force gender ("F" "M"). seed Optional integer; RNG seed CBS reproducibility. parallel Logical; use ParDNAcopy::parSegment() CBS TRUE (default). Requires ParDNAcopy package. Set parallel = FALSE use DNAcopy::segment() explicitly. cpus Integer; number threads parallel CBS (parSegment) OpenMP-accelerated steps. Default 4L.

title: RWisecondorX 0.0.0.9001 (development version)
text: package script-level reference-building CLIs now aligned strictly. build_reference.R now defaults WisecondorX references 100kb target binsize, keeps NIPTeR 50kb binsize path, errors NIPTeR BED-mode flags (--ref-binsize, --gc-table, --fasta, --separate-strands) previously accepted ignored. New nipter_control_group_qc() returns typed NIPTControlGroupQC object control-cohort diagnostics. exposes per-chromosome mean/SD/CV/Shapiro summaries, per-sample matching/outlier summaries, sex-stratified X/Y readiness metrics, optional bin-level chi-profile summaries including expected counts, scaled mean/SD/CV, chi scores, correction factors. chi-correction implementation now reuses explicit internal chi-profile builder instead recomputing discarding bin-level statistics. keeps correction path new QC/report path calculation. Gaunosome model building scoring now fail explicitly invalid reference subsets missing inputs. Sex-specific NCV/regression builders error filtered female male subset small, NCV scoring now checks requested numerator denominator chromosome columns actually present, regression sex scores centred learned mean_ratio, tied sex-model votes now warn instead silently picking class. NIPTeR S7 layer tightened around control-group abstraction. NIPTControlGroup objects now carry optional explicit sample_sex annotations plus sex_source, public nipter_as_control_group() / nipter_control_group_from_beds() builders accept labels directly. New nipter_reference_frame() builds chromosome-level training table (NChrReads_* / FrChrReads_*) directly control group, optional SampleSex column. package-level foundation future gaunosome model building instead pushing production-specific data munging core sample classes. NIPTeR modelling layer now typed S7 reference/model objects: NIPTReferenceFrame, NIPTeRSexModel, NIPTeRSexPrediction, NIPTReferenceModel. New nipter_build_reference() packages control group, reference frame, built sex models one validated reference object, nipter_predict_sex() now accepts reference object directly. nipter_build_reference() now enriches embedded NIPTReferenceFrame production-style sex-scoring columns (ConsensusGender, RR_X, RR_Y, sex-class leave-one-z-scores, IsRefSexOutlier) instead leaving gaunosome bookkeeping downstream application code. New NIPTSexScore / nipter_sex_score() score X/Y directly sex-matched non-outlier reference subset owned typed reference object. New nipter_build_sex_ncv_models() / nipter_build_sex_regression_models() attach typed gaunosome NCV regression models directly NIPTReferenceModel, nipter_ncv_sex_score() / nipter_regression_sex_score() now score X/Y package-owned reference state instead requiring loose production-script data frames model lists. New nipter_build_gaunosome_models() nipter_gaunosome_score() provide package-level gaunosome API top NIPTReferenceModel: one precomputation step X/Y NCV regression models, one typed aggregate score object bundling sex z-scores, NCV scores, regression scores, compact per-chromosome summary table. New nipter_gaunosome_report() write_nipter_gaunosome_output() provide cohort/reporting layer top typed gaunosome API: batch sample scoring now returns one validated report object flattened summary table, summary can written directly tab-separated output file without rebuilding downstream script logic. S7 implementation now exposes compatibility accessors legacy autosomal_chromosome_reads, sex_chromosome_reads, sample_name, correction-status fields, control-group fraction cache now real environment-backed cache instead dead placeholder. NIPTeR S7 compatibility layer longer registers bare $ methods upstream NIPTeR class names. fixes recursive dispatch bug overflow C stack cross-package conformance tests, keeping compatibility RWisecondorX’s namespaced S7 objects. Raw NIPTeR bin-count samples now preserve integer storage uncorrected autosomal sex matrices. corrected matrices promoted double, restores original count semantics round-trip binning tests. WisecondorX objects now use real S7 hierarchy: WisecondorXSample, WisecondorXReference, WisecondorXPrediction, WisecondorXReferenceQC validated S7 classes intentionally subclass list, existing list-style code keeps working object boundary now typed explicit. New simulate_trisomy_bam() creates BAM-level trisomy simulation donor BAM/CRAM thinning non-target chromosomes htslib-backed native implementation. preserves donor read layout sort order, writes real BAM, indexes Rduckhts. New inst/scripts/simulate_trisomy_bam.R exposes primitive CLI. New simulate_trisomy_cohort() batches donor-BAM simulator across donor set simulation grid, writes cohort manifest, reuses one Rduckhts indexing session whole run. New inst/scripts/simulate_trisomy_cohort.R exposes cohort generator  New rwisecondorx_ref_qc() mirrors upstream Python ref_qc.py heuristics native WisecondorXReference objects. returns structured PASS/WARN/FAIL report R can optionally write report JSON later inspection, without requiring Python wisecondorx package. wisecondorx_newref() now defaults cpus = 4L, matching native rwisecondorx_newref() implementation package CLI defaults. removes unnecessary single-thread slow path public Python wrapper. wisecondorx_*() CLI wrappers now isolate condathis/libmamba cache state shared ~/.cache/mamba tree setting session-local XDG_CACHE_HOME environment creation command execution. avoids stale proc.lock failures unrelated mamba sessions. Python wisecondorx_*() wrappers now cache successful condathis environment setup rest R session force condathis run known-existing working directory. avoids redundant create_env() calls fixes libmamba get current path failure prior subprocess test left R invalid deleted current directory. Python conformance tests longer convert live wisecondorx runtime failures skips. Missing optional dependencies (condathis, reticulate, numpy) still skip, environment creation, upstream wisecondorx convert/newref/predict, Python-side NPZ loading now fail loudly contextual errors. bam_convert() now keeps native dense-bin contract Rduckhts: chromosomes present BAM header come back trailing-zero-padded vectors across full header span, bam_convert_npz() adds extra upstream WisecondorX int(length / binsize + 1) NPZ padding quirk serialisation time. NPZ tests now assert exact padded lengths instead comparing shared prefixes. bundled nipter_conformance_fixture.bam now treated real package invariant rather optional convenience. generator validates every chromosome 1-22/X/Y forward reverse reads, fixture tests assert property. Live cross-package checks installed NIPTeR package now skip explicit message upstream NIPTeR::bin_bam_sample() incompatible current Rsamtools/Bioconductor stack; always-inline formula fixture tests remain strict.

Public behavior preserved: rmdup = "streaming" still matches upstream WisecondorX larp / larp2 semantics, rmdup = "flag" rmdup = "none" continue expose duplicate-flag -dedup modes. validates new native counting primitive real downstream code removes RWisecondorX’s direct dependency old SQL/file-order dedup workaround. bam_convert_npz() now accepts native filter knobs bam_convert() bam_convert_bed() (mapq, require_flags, exclude_flags). inst/scripts/convert_sample.R now forwards filters NPZ mode instead silently ignoring . nipter_bin_bam_bed() now strict bin--write convenience wrapper. what: SeparatedStrands BED expanded to 9 columns; BED readers switched to read_tabix()
title: RWisecondorX 0.0.0.9001 (development version)
text: nipter_bin_bam_bed(separate_strands = TRUE) now writes 9-column BED files: chrom, start, end, count, count_fwd, count_rev, corrected_count, corrected_fwd, corrected_rev. previous 7-column format lacked per-strand corrected values, GC-corrected SeparatedStrands samples lost per-strand correction information BED round-trip. bed_to_sample() bed_to_nipter_sample() now use read_tabix() instead read_bed() reading BED.gz files. read_tabix() returns columns VARCHAR BED-schema type coercion, avoiding problem read_bed() maps columns 7-8 thick_start/thick_end (INTEGER) rejects double-precision corrected values. bed_to_nipter_sample() format detection now uses tryCatch() around column5 probe query, correctly falls back CombinedStrands file 5 columns (column5 exist read_tabix). New tests corrected per-strand round-trip: CombinedStrands corrected values SeparatedStrands per-strand corrected values (independent forward/reverse multipliers) survive write→read cycle within floating-point tolerance. Independence forward reverse corrections explicitly verified. Fixed R CMD check NOTEs: utils::globalVariables() mclust symbols R/aaa.R; utils::write.table() qualified R/convert.R R/nipter_bin.R. Total test count: 391 assertions, passing.

what: BED.gz reader functions — close the round-trip
title: RWisecondorX 0.0.0.9001 (development version)
text: New bed_to_sample() reads 4-column BED.gz file (written bam_convert_bed()) back named-list--integer-vectors format expected rwisecondorx_newref() rwisecondorx_predict(). closes WisecondorX round-trip: bin bam_convert_bed(), store BED.gz, reload analysis without re-reading BAM. New bed_to_nipter_sample() reads 5-column (CombinedStrands) 9-column (SeparatedStrands) BED.gz file (written nipter_bin_bam_bed()) NIPTeRSample object compatible NIPTeR statistical functions. Column count auto-detected. Handles literal "NA" strings corrected_count field via TRY_CAST. Sample name inferred filename set explicitly. functions accept optional DuckDB connection reuse across multiple files, creating one internally (allow_unsigned_extensions = "true") none supplied. New inst/tinytest/test_bed_reader.R — 46 assertions covering WisecondorX 4-column round-trip, NIPTeR CombinedStrands 5-column round-trip, SeparatedStrands 9-column round-trip (four matrices), corrected per-strand round-trip, sample name inference, integration scale_sample(). Total test count: 391 assertions (46 test_bed_reader.R), passing.

title: RWisecondorX 0.0.0.9001 (development version)
text: New rwisecondorx_newref() — pure-R/Rcpp implementation WisecondorX newref pipeline. Takes list binned samples (bam_convert()) builds PCA-based reference: gender model training (2-component GMM Y-fractions), global bin masking, per-partition normalize/PCA/filter/KNN reference building, null ratio computation. Supports NIPT mode, custom Y-fraction cutoff, multi-threaded KNN via OpenMP. New rwisecondorx_predict() — pure-R implementation WisecondorX predict pipeline. Coverage normalization, PCA projection, iterative within-sample normalization aberration masking, gonosomal normalization, result inflation, log-transformation, optional blacklist masking, CBS segmentation (via DNAcopy ParDNAcopy), segment Z-scoring, aberration calling. Supports Z-score beta/ratio calling modes. New rwisecondorx_write_bins_bed(), rwisecondorx_write_segments_bed(), rwisecondorx_write_aberrations_bed(), rwisecondorx_write_statistics() writing prediction results BED statistics files. New scale_sample() rescaling binned samples different bin sizes (e.g. 5kb → 100kb). R/rwisecondorx_utils.R — shared utilities including .train_gender_model() (mclust NULL fallback zero-variance Y-fractions -female cohorts), .gender_correct(), .get_mask(), .normalize_and_mask(), .train_pca(), .project_pc(), .predict_gender(). R/rwisecondorx_cbs.R — .exec_cbs() wrapper around DNAcopy’s segment() upstream WisecondorX conventions (0→NA, 0 weights→1e-99, split segments large NA gaps, recalculate weighted means). Supports ParDNAcopy parallel segmentation.

what: Rcpp acceleration for KNN reference building
title: RWisecondorX 0.0.0.9001 (development version)
text: New src/knn_reference.cpp implementing knn_reference_cpp() null_ratios_cpp() C++ OpenMP parallelization. Replaces O(n_bins² × n_samples) pure-R double -loop compiled code. KNN reference-finding step previously took minutes now completes seconds. Rcpp added Imports LinkingTo DESCRIPTION. OpenMP flags src/Makevars src/Makevars.win.

what: Fix: KNN index semantics in predict normalization
title: RWisecondorX 0.0.0.9001 (development version)
text: Fixed correctness bug .normalize_once() global KNN indexes (stored knn_reference_cpp()) used directly index chr_data, leave-one-chromosome-subset. Global index g now correctly mapped local space: g excluded chromosome, g - n_chr . bug caused reference bins looked wrong positions within-sample normalization. Note: upstream WisecondorX inverse issue — stores LOCAL indexes newref uses GLOBAL indexes null_ratio computation. implementation stores GLOBAL indexes now correctly handles predict (local conversion) null_ratio (global direct use).

what: Fix: null ratio column count mismatch
title: RWisecondorX 0.0.0.9001 (development version)
text: Fixed rwisecondorx_predict() crash autosomal sub-reference (“”) gonosomal sub-reference (“F”) different numbers null-ratio columns (due different sample counts per partition). now truncated min(ncol(aut), ncol(gon)) rbind().

what: Fix: mclust GMM on zero-variance Y-fractions
title: RWisecondorX 0.0.0.9001 (development version)
text: .train_gender_model() now handles case female samples exactly 0.0 Y-fraction (Y reads). mclust::Mclust(..., G=2) returns NULL scenario; fix falls back gap-based cutoff: min(nonzero_y_fractions) / 2.

title: RWisecondorX 0.0.0.9001 (development version)
text: New generate_cohort() creates synthetic BAM files testing using “compressed” chromosome lengths (100bp per bin instead 100kb). Produces identical bin COUNT structure GRCh37 100kb resolution ~435KB BAMs. Supports injecting trisomy signal (extra reads target chromosome). New COMPRESSED_BINSIZE constant (100L) exported use bam_convert(binsize = COMPRESSED_BINSIZE). inst/scripts/make_cohort.R — CLI wrapper cohort generation.

title: RWisecondorX 0.0.0.9001 (development version)
text: test files now use library(RWisecondorX) instead source() hacks load R code directly. required functions call compiled C++ code (knn_reference_cpp, null_ratios_cpp) available installed package. Fixed tinytest assertion: expect_message(..., pattern = NA) mean “expect messages” — fails message emitted. Replaced expect_silent(). New inst/tinytest/test_rwisecondorx.R — 76 assertions covering reference building (gender model, masking, PCA, KNN indexes, null ratios), prediction (normalization, CBS, aberration calling), trisomy detection sensitivity. New inst/tinytest/test_cohort_pipeline.R — 31 assertions end--end pipeline: cohort generation → binning → reference building → prediction → trisomy detection. Tests trisomy 21, 18, 13 detected gains euploid samples produce aberrations. Total test count: 345 assertions, passing.

what: Y-unique region ratio for sex prediction
title: RWisecondorX 0.0.0.9001 (development version)
text: New nipter_y_unique_ratio() counts reads overlapping 7 Y-chromosome unique gene regions (HSFY1, BPY2, BPY2B, BPY2C, XKRY, PRY, PRY2) computes ratio total nuclear genome reads. Uses DuckDB/duckhts index-based region queries (read_bam(region := ...)) efficient BAM access. bundled GRCh37 regions file can replaced custom file assemblies. New nipter_sex_model_y_unique() fits 2-component GMM Y-unique ratios (one per BAM cohort), producing NIPTeRSexModel compatible nipter_predict_sex(). nipter_predict_sex() gains y_unique_ratio parameter passing pre-computed Y-unique ratio "y_unique" model included consensus vote. enables full 3-model majority-vote sex prediction pipeline (Y-unique ratio + Y fraction + XY fractions). Bundled inst/extdata/grch37_Y_UniqueRegions.txt — TSV 7 GRCh37 Y-unique regions used Y-unique ratio calculation.

what: Sex prediction via Gaussian mixture models
title: RWisecondorX 0.0.0.9001 (development version)
text: New nipter_sex_model() fits 2-component GMM sex chromosome fractions NIPTeRControlGroup using mclust::Mclust(). Supports "y_fraction" (univariate Y-chromosome fraction) "xy_fraction" (bivariate X + Y fractions) methods. male cluster identified component higher median Y fraction. New nipter_predict_sex() classifies NIPTeRSample male female given one NIPTeRSexModel objects. Multiple models use majority vote consensus (tie defaults “female” — conservative NIPT). mclust added Suggests DESCRIPTION.

title: RWisecondorX 0.0.0.9001 (development version)
text: nipter_bin_bam_bed() gains separate_strands parameter. TRUE, outputs 9-column BED (chrom, start, end, count, count_fwd, count_rev, corrected_count, corrected_fwd, corrected_rev) count = count_fwd + count_rev. FALSE (default), 5-column BED format unchanged.

title: RWisecondorX 0.0.0.9001 (development version)
text: bam_convert() gains separate_strands parameter. TRUE, returns list fwd (forward strand) rev (reverse strand) data frames. nipter_bin_bam() gains separate_strands = TRUE support, producing NIPTeRSample objects class c("NIPTeRSample", "SeparatedStrands"). Autosomal chromosome reads stored list two matrices (forward reverse) rownames "1F".."22F" "1R".."22R". NIPTeR statistical functions now support SeparatedStrands samples: nipter_gc_correct(): LOESS/bin-weight fitted summed strand counts, corrections applied independently strand matrix. nipter_chi_correct(): chi-squared computed summed strands, correction applied per-strand. nipter_z_score() nipter_ncv_score(): use collapsed (F+R) fractions. nipter_regression(): doubles predictor pool (44 candidates: "1F".."22F", "1R".."22R") complementary exclusion within model (selecting "5F" excludes "5F" "5R").

title: RWisecondorX 0.0.0.9001 (development version)
text: wisecondorx_predict(): removed hallucinated ref_binsize / --binsize parameter exist upstream predict subcommand. wisecondorx_newref(): fixed ref_binsize default 50000L 100000L match upstream default. wisecondorx_predict(): added add_plot_title parameter mapping upstream --add-plot-title.
